title: agent-runtime: process isolation, sandboxing, and network policy
issueBody:
## Summary
No process isolation in agent-runtime:

### Local Executor
Spawns with full parent env access. No namespaces, chroot, seccomp, or capabilities restriction. Child processes can read/write host filesystem, access credentials, consume unbounded resources.

### Network
No network policy enforcement, DNS filtering, proxy/firewall integration, or subnet isolation.

### SSH/Docker gaps
- SSH: no host key verification (StrictHostKeyChecking=no), no retry, no pooling
- Docker: no daemon availability check, no image pull verification, no resource limits (--cpus/--memory)

Ref: `docs/agent-layer-gaps.md`

comments.author: tmuskal
comments.body:
**Related:** #585 (K8s executor needs sandboxing)
**Related:** #593 (background process state transitions need isolation awareness)

comments.author: a5c-ai
comments.body: Triage summary: agent-runtime lacks a shared execution policy/sandboxing layer, leaving local, Docker, SSH, and background execution with unsafe defaults around environment, filesystem, network, and resources. Root cause: execution is thin adapters around child_process.spawn, docker run, and ssh with no shared sandbox or policy abstraction. Recommended approach: add explicit execution policy model covering env allow/deny lists, filesystem mounts/bounds, network mode/egress policy, OS resource limits, sandbox mode, Docker security/preflight behavior, and SSH host-key policy. Wire through ExecutionConfig and all executors. Make local execution clearly unsafe-by-default or restricted-by-policy. Update tests to assert secure defaults and explicit opt-in for insecure legacy behavior.

relatedIssues.title: agent-runtime: crash recovery, persistent queue, K8s executor
relatedIssues.relationship: Related but not duplicate. K8s executor needs sandboxing, but persistent queue/crash recovery is out of scope for issue #590.

relatedIssues.title: agent-runtime: background process gaps — state transitions, signals, backpressure
relatedIssues.relationship: Related but not duplicate. Background process lifecycle states/signals/backpressure are broader than #590, but #590 should add isolation-aware policy hooks and output/resource caps where needed.

reuseAuditFindings.processLibrary: No .a5c/process-library/ directory found. Existing process patterns reviewed from .a5c/processes/issue-181-run-completed-idempotency.mjs and .a5c/processes/issue-485-sonnet-proxy-tool-translation.mjs.
matchingInfrastructure: packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/types.ts defines ExecutionConfig unions without policy fields.
packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/modes/local.ts uses child_process.spawn and currently inherits process.env.
packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/modes/docker.ts builds docker run arguments but lacks security/resource/preflight policy.
packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/modes/ssh.ts builds ssh arguments and currently disables strict host-key checking.
packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/modes/kubernetes.ts builds a structural Job manifest and can map policy to manifest fields where possible.
packages/agent-runtime/src/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts spawns shell background processes with merged parent env and unbounded output buffers.
packages/agent-runtime/src/resources/manager.ts tracks logical budgets but does not enforce OS resource limits.
packages/agent-runtime/src/execution/__tests__/provider.test.ts currently tests Docker/SSH command construction and encodes insecure SSH behavior.
docs/agent-layer-gaps.md documents the L5 process isolation, Docker, SSH, resource, and background-process gaps.
noMatchingExistingInfrastructureFoundFor: SSH known_hosts or pinned host-key policy
OS-level CPU/memory/pids enforcement connected to ResourceManager

acceptanceCriteria: ExecutionConfig exposes a shared, typed execution policy model for env, filesystem, network, resources, sandbox behavior, Docker, SSH, and Kubernetes-compatible fields.
Local execution no longer inherits parent environment by default and fails fast for requested isolation guarantees it cannot enforce.
Docker execution has deterministic secure defaults, preflight seams, resource limit flags, read-only/cap/security/user/network/DNS policy support, and explicit insecure opt-ins.
SSH execution verifies host keys by default and only emits StrictHostKeyChecking=no under an explicit insecure opt-in.
Background process spawning uses the same env/cwd/resource/output policy semantics where in scope and exposes output truncation/limit metadata.
ResourceManager has a coherent policy/admission seam for OS resource limits without absorbing issue #585 crash recovery scope.
Tests cover secure defaults, explicit insecure legacy opt-ins, unsupported local guarantees, and Docker/SSH command construction without requiring live Docker/SSH/Kubernetes dependencies.
Documentation records secure defaults, migration behavior, local limitations, and remaining unsupported enforcement guarantees.
